question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the null check on contractTransactionPredicate (line 140) so that when the predicate is null, addContractTransaction() returns early without recording the contract transaction, causing a contract interaction to be silently omitted from the contractTransactions map and missing from the persisted contract transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user trigger addContractTransaction() with an EntityId that passes the contractTransactionPredicate but whose getId() returns a value already present in the contractTransactions map (line 143, computeIfAbsent), causing the existing entry to be silently preserved and a duplicate contract interaction to be dropped from history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that produces a large number of contract transactions so that populateContractTransactions() (line 266-268) sets the same list of all contract IDs on every ContractTransaction entry, causing the persisted contractIds list to include unrelated contract IDs and corrupting the contract interaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that setEntityNftTransactionPredicate() at line 159-161 appends a filter excluding payerAccountId, so that by controlling which account is the payer, the attacker can ensure their own account is excluded from NFT entity transaction records, causing the payer's NFT transfer history to be silently omitted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged user pass a null predicate to setEntityNftTransactionPredicate() (line 159, predicate != null check) to reset entityNftTransactionPredicate to REJECT_ALL, causing all subsequent addNftTransactionEntityId() calls to be silently dropped and NFT entity transaction history to be erased for that RecordItem?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEntityTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged user pass a null predicate to setEntityTransactionPredicate() (line 163-165) to reset entityTransactionPredicate to REJECT_ALL, causing all addEntityId() calls to be silently rejected and the entity transaction history for that RecordItem to be completely suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where the entityTransactionBuilder (line 172-177) is initialized with a stale consensusTimestamp or wrong transactionType from a previous call, causing subsequent computeIfAbsent() calls (line 180) to record entity transactions with incorrect metadata and corrupting the entity transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that calls doAddEntityId() with an EntityId whose getId() collides with an existing key in the entityTransactions map (line 180, computeIfAbsent), causing the first-recorded entity transaction to persist and a later legitimate entity interaction to be silently dropped from history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged user trigger concurrent processing of a RecordItem where logIndex is lazily initialized (line 193-196) in a non-atomic way, causing two threads to both see logIndex == null and both initialize it to new AtomicInteger(0), resulting in duplicate log index 0 being assigned to two different contract logs and corrupting the log ordering in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that generates a very large number of contract logs, causing getAndIncrementLogIndex() to increment the AtomicInteger (line 196) past Integer.MAX_VALUE and overflow to negative values, causing contract logs to be recorded with negative indices and disrupting the log ordering in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with parentConsensusTimestamp set (line 204, hasParentConsensusTimestamp()) but pointing to a non-existent parent, causing isChild() to return true while parseParent() returns null, creating an inconsistency where the transaction is classified as a child but has no parent reference, corrupting the child/parent classification in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isInvalidIdError()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that returns INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID (line 209) to cause isInvalidIdError() to return true, triggering special handling in the importer that skips entity creation, and by doing so prevent a legitimate entity from being recorded in history, effectively erasing it from the entity transaction index?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where hookParent is non-null (line 272-274) by manipulating parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to return a wrong sibling, causing getContractRelatedParent() to return that wrong sibling as the contract-related parent and misattributing contract results to the wrong parent transaction in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction where transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp() produces a value that overflows DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() (line 305), causing consensusTimestamp to wrap to a very small or negative value and the transaction to be inserted at the wrong position in the time-ordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction where transactionBody.getTransactionID().getAccountID() resolves to EntityId.EMPTY (line 308), causing payerAccountId to be EMPTY and all subsequent payer-based filtering (e.g., setEntityNftTransactionPredicate exclusion) to behave incorrectly, corrupting the payer attribution in the recorded transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged user submit two transactions with identical consensusTimestamp values (timestamp collision) so that parentTimestamp.equals(previous.transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp()) at line 333 matches the wrong transaction, causing a child to be linked to an unintended parent and reorganizing the transaction history tree?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that parseParent() uses Timestamp.equals() (protobuf equality, line 333) rather than a numeric nanosecond comparison, so that two Timestamp objects with the same nanosecond value but different internal representations could fail equality, causing a valid parent to be missed and the child to be recorded as parentless?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user submit a long chain of sibling transactions all with non-null ContractFunctionResults and contractNum != 365, causing the while loop in parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() (line 359-371) to traverse the entire previous chain without finding a hook parent, resulting in O(n) traversal that could be exploited to cause processing delays and out-of-order history recording?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where the while loop condition candidateRecord != parent (line 359) is never satisfied because parent is null, causing the loop to traverse the entire previous chain until candidateRecord becomes null, potentially processing stale sibling data and assigning a wrong hookParent from a previous block's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that parseSuccess() at line 391 only checks the immediate parent's isSuccessful() and not the full ancestor chain, so that a grandchild transaction with a failed grandparent but a successful (incorrectly resolved) parent is recorded as successful, corrupting the cascading failure propagation in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with status SUCCESS_BUT_MISSING_EXPECTED_OPERATION (line 398) to cause parseSuccess() to return true, causing the mirror node to record a partially-failed transaction as fully successful and misrepresenting the transaction outcome in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: transactionIndex field] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a crafted transactionIndex value (line 82, transactionIndex field) that conflicts with an existing transaction's index within the same block, causing the mirror node to record two transactions at the same index position and disrupting the block-level transaction ordering in history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: sidecarRecords field] Can an unprivileged user exploit the public setSidecarRecords() setter (line 120) on a @NonFinal field to replace the sidecar records of a RecordItem after it has been built but before it is processed, causing the importer to process different sidecar data than what was originally in the stream and corrupting the contract state change history?
